name: putValueToControl
title: putValueToControl

description: Assigns the value val to the Panel Designer Multi Display Control or the control:
The Multi Display Control/ CAPL Output View is located on the panel with the title panel.

Different contents are displayed with the Multi Display Control/CAPL Output View. In addition to numbers (float and integer) and texts (char[]), in particular different messages (CAN, LIN, ... and J1939 PGNs) can also be displayed.

parameters.name: panel
parameters.description: Name of the panel, restricted to 128 characters.

parameters.name: control
parameters.description: Name of the control, restricted to 128 characters.

parameters.name: Note This parameter is only available with the: CAPL Output View

parameters.description: In this case, no new paragraph is set. The text will be append directly to the existing output. This is the default setting. If you want to use the default setting you do not need to set the parameter when calling the function.
In this case, the output is written to a new line.

parameters.name: Note This parameter is only available with the: CAPL Output View The physical value of a signal cannot be displayed in hexadecimal format. Then the setting is not considered and will be ignored. Only the raw value of a signal can be formatted hexadecimal.

parameters.description: In this case, the signal/message/value is written in decimal notation. For signals this is the default setting. If you want to use the default setting you do not need to set the parameter when calling the function.

In this case, the signal/message/value is written in hexadecimal notation. For messages this is the default setting. If you want to use the default setting you do not need to set the parameter when calling the function.

description: Example: Output of a string, a message, a signal - no additional function parameters used
Example: Output of a message — optional with function parameters
Example: Output of a signal (raw format) — optional with function parameters
Example: Output of a signal (physical format) — optional with function parameters

availability: 3.2 7.5: This function requires CANoe 7.5 and higher.1
